passages.passage_text: Each questions comprises two statements (numbered as I and II). You have to take the statements as true even, if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follow from the given statements, disregarding commonly known facts.

In each of the questions below are given some statements followed by some conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some beds are mirrors. Some mirrors are dolls. Some dolls are cheques. Some cheques are pins. Conclusions: I. Some pins are dolls. II. Some cheques are beds. III. Some cheques are mirrors. IV. Some dolls are beds.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All chocolates are holders. No holder is a lamp. Some lamps are desks. All desks are pens. Conclusions: I. Some pens are holders. II. Some desks are lamps. III. No pen is a holder. IV. Some pens are chocolates.

choices.e: Either I or III and II follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: All glasses are rooms. Some rooms are planes. All planes are ducks. Some ducks are lanterns. Conclusions: I. Some lanterns are planes. II. Some ducks are rooms. III. Some rooms are glasses. IV. Some ducks are glasses.

choices.d: All I, II, III and IV follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some sweets are almonds. Some almonds are cashews. All cashews are pistachios. Conclusions: I. All almonds are pistachios. II. Some pistachios are sweets. III. Some sweets are cashews. IV. All almonds are either sweets or cashews.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some fruits are seeds. All seeds are trees. All plants are trees. Conclusions: I. Some plants are seeds. II. Some plants are fruits. III. Some trees are fruits. IV. No plant is a seed.

choices.b: III and either I or IV follow
choices.c: II and either I or IV follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: I. All contracts are agreements. II. All agreements are accepted offers. Which of the following derivations is correct?

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: I. Some beautiful women are actresses. II. All actresses are good dancers. Which of the following derivations is correct?

choices.a: Some beautiful women are good dancers.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statements: Some animals are fighters. All fighters are tigers. No tiger is lighter. Conclusions: I. None of the lighters is tiger. II. Some tigers are definitely not lighters. III. Most of the fighters are animals.
